data.title: GoldMining Lifts La Mina Project NPV to $1 Billion in Updated PEA
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

GoldMining ( (TSE:GOLD) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 28, 2026, GoldMining released an updated preliminary economic assessment for its La Mina gold-copper project in Antioquia, Colombia, showing a 265% jump in after-tax NPV at a 5% discount rate versus its prior study. Using updated base case prices of $3,500/oz gold, $4.70/lb copper and $40/oz silver, the study outlines an after-tax NPV of $1.0 billion, a 32.2% IRR and a 2.7-year payback on initial capital of $523 million.

The PEA envisions a conventional 15,000 tpd open-pit operation producing an average 152,400 oz of gold equivalent annually in the first five years and 1.5 million oz over an 11.2-year mine life at AISC of $1,045/oz gold on a by-product basis. At current spot prices, the after-tax NPV rises to about $1.8 billion with a 49.1% IRR, underscoring La Mina’s leverage to higher metals prices and reinforcing the project’s potential importance within GoldMining’s broader resource portfolio, while remaining subject to the usual uncertainties associated with PEA-level, resource-based economics.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:GOLD) stock is a Buy with a C$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GoldMining stock, see the TSE:GOLD Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GOLD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GOLD is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance (no revenue, persistent losses, and ongoing cash burn) despite a conservatively levered balance sheet. Technical indicators are mixed but lean soft (negative MACD and below key moving averages). Valuation is also pressured because losses drive a negative P/E and there is no dividend yield support.

To see Spark’s full report on GOLD stock, click here.

More about GoldMining

GoldMining Inc. is a Vancouver-based precious and base metals developer focused on advancing a portfolio of gold, copper and silver projects across the Americas. The company targets large-scale, potentially low-cost deposits and currently reports significant measured, indicated and inferred resources, with the La Mina gold-copper project in Colombia forming a key component of its growth pipeline.

Average Trading Volume: 542,860

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$351M

data.description:

Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson ( (ERIC) ) just unveiled an update.

Between April 20 and April 24, 2026, Ericsson repurchased 2.4 million of its own Class B shares on Nasdaq Stockholm under its previously announced share buyback program of up to SEK 15 billion, at a corrected average price of SEK 105.69 and a total value of about SEK 253.7 million. The purchases, executed by Goldman Sachs Bank Europe SE and carried out under EU market abuse and safe-harbour rules, lifted Ericsson’s Class B treasury stock to 40.4 million shares out of 3.37 billion total shares, supporting capital management ahead of a planned proposal to cancel most of the repurchased stock at the 2027 AGM, which could enhance earnings per share and underscore management’s confidence in the company’s long-term prospects.

data.description:

Hycroft Mining Holding ( (HYMC) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 28, 2026, Hycroft Mining reported its first-quarter 2026 results, highlighting an injury-free safety record with more than 1.4 million work hours without a lost-time incident and a strong balance sheet holding $189 million in cash and no debt. The company also disclosed a roughly 55% increase in measured and indicated gold and silver resources, the establishment of an initial high-grade underground silver resource, and index inclusions that signal rising market visibility.

Operationally, Hycroft continued its 2025–2026 exploration drill program at the Brimstone and Vortex high-grade silver systems, completing more than 9,000 meters of drilling and extending high-grade mineralization at both zones. For the remainder of 2026, the miner plans to add drill rigs, accelerate exploration, advance economic and processing studies—including a preliminary economic assessment and process trade-off work—and complete drilling and metallurgical tests that could support a potential restart of heap leach operations and lay groundwork for future development.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (losses, no recent revenue, and ongoing cash burn), partially offset by strong technical momentum and a set of positive project-focused corporate developments. Valuation provides limited support due to negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on HYMC stock, click here.

More about Hycroft Mining Holding

Hycroft Mining Holding Corporation is a U.S.-based gold and silver company focused on exploring and developing the Hycroft Mine in northern Nevada, one of the world’s largest precious metals deposits. After a long history of oxide heap leach operations, the company is conducting a robust 2025–2026 drill program and technical studies to transition the mine to milling sulfide mineralization and to expand two newly discovered high-grade silver systems.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

BP ( (BP) ) has shared an announcement.

BP reported its group results for the first quarter of 2026 in a Form 6-K for the three months ended 31 March 2026, highlighting “continued strong operational and financial delivery” after a volatile prior year. Profit attributable to shareholders swung to $3.84 billion from a $3.42 billion loss in the fourth quarter of 2025 and was sharply higher than the $687 million earned in the first quarter of 2025, while replacement cost profit reached $662 million versus a $2.76 billion loss in the previous quarter.

The filing, which also includes management’s discussion and analysis, full consolidated financial statements, legal proceedings and a recent credit ratings update, is incorporated by reference into multiple existing BP securities registration statements. This underscores the importance of the quarter’s rebound in profitability for bondholders and equity investors, as it feeds directly into the disclosure base used for BP’s funding activities and may influence market perceptions of its credit quality and balance sheet resilience.

data.title: Novartis Q1 2026: Growth Brands and Pipeline Advance Despite Generic Headwinds
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Novartis ( (NVS) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 28, 2026, Novartis reported first-quarter 2026 results showing net sales slipping 5% at constant currencies to USD 13.1 billion, as strong volume growth and surging priority brands were more than offset by U.S. generic erosion and pricing pressure. Core operating income fell 14% at constant currencies and net income declined 13%, although free cash flow held broadly steady at USD 3.3 billion, and the company reaffirmed full-year 2026 guidance for low single-digit net sales growth and a low single-digit decline in core operating income.

The quarter underscored the strength of Novartis’s growth drivers, with Kisqali, Pluvicto, Kesimpta, Scemblix, Leqvio and Fabhalta all posting double‑digit to triple‑digit constant‑currency gains, partially counterbalancing declines in older products such as the Zolgensma group. Novartis advanced its R&D pipeline with positive late-stage data for remibrutinib and Fabhalta, new regulatory milestones for Cosentyx and ianalumab, and completed the acquisition of Avidity to add three late-stage neuromuscular medicines, steps that support its long‑term growth ambitions despite near‑term margin pressure from higher R&D spending and generic competition.

The most recent analyst rating on (NVS) stock is a Buy with a $140.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Novartis stock, see the NVS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NVS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NVS is a Outperform.

Score is driven primarily by strong underlying financial quality (high margins and strong free cash flow) and a bullish technical trend. The key constraints are management’s softer 2026 profit outlook amid significant LOE/generic headwinds and a valuation that looks reasonable rather than clearly discounted.

To see Spark’s full report on NVS stock, click here.

More about Novartis

Novartis is a pure-play innovative medicines company headquartered in Basel, Switzerland, focused on four core therapeutic areas: cardiovascular-renal-metabolic, immunology, neuroscience and oncology. It prioritizes established technology platforms in chemistry and biotherapeutics, alongside emerging platforms such as gene and cell therapy, radioligand therapy and xRNA, with a commercial focus on the U.S., China, Germany and Japan.

TransUnion reported strong first-quarter 2026 results on April 28, 2026, with revenue rising 14% year over year to $1.25 billion, or 11% on an organic constant-currency basis, led by 24% growth in U.S. Financial Services and solid gains across most international markets. data.title: Petrobras Moves to 98% Control of Jubarte With Argonauta Ring-Fence Deal
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras ( (PBR) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 27, 2026, Petrobras announced an agreement to acquire 100% of a portion of the Argonauta Field ring‑fence in the Campos Basin from Shell, ONGC, and Brava, covering 0.86% of the shared pre‑salt Jubarte reservoir under a unitization deal effective since August 1, 2025. The transaction, valued at R$ 700 million plus US$ 150 million to be paid in three installments and subject to ANP and CADE approval, will raise Petrobras’s interest in the Jubarte Shared Reservoir to 98.11%, streamline asset management and equalization talks, and further consolidate its strategic position and scale in the high‑productivity Parque das Baleias cluster, which currently produces about 210,000 barrels of oil per day.

The most recent analyst rating on (PBR) stock is a Buy with a $24.80 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras stock, see the PBR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PBR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PBR is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (strong margins and cash generation, though cyclical and less robust than peak years) and strong technical trend signals despite overbought momentum risk. Valuation is a major positive with a very low P/E and high dividend yield, while the earnings call supports the outlook via operational execution but flags key risks from oil-price exposure (no hedging) and elevated debt/leasing obligations.

To see Spark’s full report on PBR stock, click here.

More about Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras

Petróleo Brasileiro S.A. – Petrobras is Brazil’s state-controlled oil and gas company, operating across exploration, production, refining, and distribution, with a strong focus on offshore pre-salt reserves. The group is a dominant player in the Brazilian energy market, particularly in the Campos Basin, where it runs major deepwater and ultra-deepwater production hubs such as Parque das Baleias.

data.title: Spotify Posts Record Q1 2026 Profitability as Users Reach 761 Million
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Spotify ( (SPOT) ) has issued an update.

On April 28, 2026, Spotify reported its Q1 2026 results, showing total monthly active users rising to 761 million and premium subscribers to 293 million, representing year-on-year growth of 12% and 9% respectively. Management highlighted that these user metrics met or exceeded guidance, underscoring continued momentum in both paid and ad-supported audiences.

Quarterly revenue reached €4.53 billion, up 8% year-on-year, or 14% in constant currency, with premium revenue growing 10% and ad-supported revenue declining 5% on a reported basis. Despite FX headwinds and weaker reported ad performance, Spotify delivered a Q1 record gross margin of 33.0% and record operating income of €715 million, supported by disciplined costs and lower-than-expected social charges.

Free cash flow also hit a Q1 record at €824 million, bringing last-twelve-month free cash flow to approximately €3.2 billion and leaving the company with €8.8 billion in cash and short-term investments at quarter-end. The reclassification of some revenue-generating activities from the ad-supported to premium segment, following the appointment of new co-chief executives, signals an operational reshaping of how Spotify manages and reports its businesses.

Product-wise, Spotify advanced its AI-led strategy with beta launches of Taste Profile and Prompted Playlist for premium users and rolled out new features like About the Song in select markets and SongDNA globally to enrich music context. It also expanded its audiobook offering with new charts in the U.S. and U.K., moves that aim to deepen engagement and diversify content consumption across its platform.

The most recent analyst rating on (SPOT) stock is a Buy with a $650.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Spotify stock, see the SPOT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SPOT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SPOT is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by materially improved profitability, margins, and strong free cash flow, reinforced by constructive Q1 2026 guidance and management’s expectation for further margin and cash flow gains. These positives are tempered by very weak technicals (sharp downtrend with negative momentum) and a premium valuation that leaves less room for execution missteps, alongside near-term risks around advertising softness, FX headwinds, and potential churn from pricing.

To see Spark’s full report on SPOT stock, click here.

More about Spotify

Spotify Technology S.A. is a global audio streaming company operating in the digital media and technology industry, best known for its music, podcast and audiobook platforms. The company monetizes through premium subscriptions and advertising-supported tiers, with a large international user base and a strategic focus on AI-driven personalization and content discovery.

The latest announcement is out from ASA Gold and Precious Metals Limited ( (ASA) ).

ASA Gold and Precious Metals Limited is in the process of selling part of its stake in a privately held portfolio company through multiple transactions at varying prices, with each sale recognized upon closing. The investment is currently valued using the most recent observable transaction price, and the company estimates that, if these deals are completed at the anticipated levels, they could lift its net asset value by roughly 2–3%, though there is no assurance the transactions will occur as expected, underscoring some uncertainty for shareholders around the final outcome.

The potential boost to net asset value highlights the importance of this private holding within ASA’s portfolio and signals that successful execution could marginally enhance the fund’s financial position. However, the conditional nature of the planned sales means investors must weigh the prospective benefit against the risk that pricing or completion may differ from current expectations, which could temper the anticipated impact on the company’s overall valuation.
